experiences.title: Executive-Design
experiences.headline: Campaign ideation and visuals, social media content, video editing, motion graphics, print assets.
experiences.summary: Led the creative direction for Anil Agarwal Foundation, Nand Ghar, and Animal Care Organization, overseeing the complete design ecosystem from social media and print collateral to integrated campaign concepts and visual storytelling. Partnered with stakeholders to translate social impact goals into engaging creative solutions, ensuring cohesive brand communication across digital, print, and experiential platforms.
experiences.impact: 2x Donations, 500M+ cross platform views, 1.5x social media growth

projects.title: 'KhanaKhayaKya' Campaign
projects.tools: Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere pro, After Effects, Indesign
projects.summary: #KhaanaKhaayaKya (Did you eat?) was an awareness campaign by the Anil Agarwal Foundation that aimed to address child hunger and malnutrition by encouraging public conversations around nutrition, food security, and the importance of ensuring every child has access to healthy meals.
projects.whatPrernaDid: Designed and delivered high-impact visual assets for the campaign, crafting emotionally driven storytelling and awareness-focused creatives that amplified conversations around child nutrition, hunger eradication, and community engagement across digital platforms.

finalOutput.label: Paid Ads
finalOutput.helper: Finished campaign visuals, edited videos, final posts, approved layouts, or production-ready assets.

projects.title: 'HunarSePehchaan' Campaign
projects.tools: Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere pro, After Effects
projects.summary:
#HunarSePehchaan (Recognition Through Skills) was an International Women’s Day campaign by the Anil Agarwal Foundation that celebrated women whose identities are shaped by their talent, skills, and determination. The campaign highlighted the importance of skill development, self-reliance, and economic empowerment, encouraging society to recognize women not just for who they are, but for what they achieve through their capabilities.

Background:
Through the skill development initiatives for women at Nand Ghar, CSR Projects at Vedanta Foundation, and CSR Projects at Vedanta Business Units (TARA, FACOR Sathi Jivika, Project Sakhi) etc, Vedanta and Anil Agarwal Foundation are creating opportunities for self-reliance and independence. They are creating sustainable livelihoods and driving a positive change in the communities. These initiatives also align with this year’s UN theme for International Women’s Day, ‘Invest in Women: Accelerate Progress,’ which highlights investing in women to create equal opportunities as an important step to achieve gender equality.

Insight:
Beyond income, the women who have gained skills from these initiatives have also gained more confidence and feel more respected in the community. They are known for the work that they’re doing. Their skills have given them an identity. 

Messaging:
The messaging will highlight the role of skill development and financial independence in women’s empowerment. Through human-interest stories, data, and user interactions, we will emphasize on the importance of creating access to upgrading women’s knowledge as the first step to creating better livelihoods and enabling them to lead more fulfilling lives.
projects.whatPrernaDid: Developed the creative direction for the Hunar Se Pehchaan campaign by creating campaign concepts, moodboards, and visual storytelling frameworks. Produced logo animations and narrative-driven content that showcased the impact of Anil Agarwal Foundation and Nand Ghar's initiatives in women's skill development, empowerment, and community transformation across digital platforms.

portfolioProjects.impact: Shows personal style, detail, product thinking, and client-led creative execution

about.headline: Graphic designer with campaign discipline and a handmade visual voice.
body: Prerna creates brand visuals, campaign assets, motion graphics, social content, illustration, and digital storytelling work. Her project showcase connects practical production skills with expressive, human visual details.
Recruiters can use the Work section to open each experience and see what she created there: campaign graphics, motion pieces, social layouts, illustration-led products, and brand systems.
